title: trichera dipsacifolia subsp. pocutica
intro: Trichera dipsacifolia subsp. pocutica, also known as Pocutian Yarrow or Pocutian Trichera, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb native to Eastern Europe and is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Trichera dipsacifolia subsp. pocutica include relief from digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce fever. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies for wound healing and skin conditions.
